Imports are definitionally included in C + G+ I, so it is true that they are neutral in the equation.  If I buy a TV made in Korea, it counts as a + for C and a - for M.  

Given this, let me then be more precise and say that for a given level of C + G + I, imports decrease GDP.

author: GavinSBaker
text: This is not the best way to frame an effective argument against tariffs IMO.

author: GavinSBaker
text:
In the most literal sense possible, imports subtract from GDP.  It is a 4 term equation where GDP = C + G + I + (X-M) with M being imports.

There are super compelling arguments against tariffs.  This is not one of them.

author: GavinSBaker
text:
Not that there is the slightest bit of logic to being skeptical of Generative AI because the Metaverse and Web 3.0 have been busts thus far.

But logic rarely matters for opinions and this  is a surprisingly common opinion.

author: GavinSBaker
text:
For those skeptical of Generative AI because Web 3.0 and the Metaverse have been busts so far:

ChatGPT has 4x more search interest than peak Ethereum, 30x peak Metaverse and 50x peak Web 3.0.

Product-market fit unseen since the iPhone at a minimum.

Thought via 
@benedictevans

author: GavinSBaker
text:
They have so many advantages if they ever wake up.

More data, more compute and more distribution than anyone.

They *should* win on paper.

Just need the right culture and management.  Unfortunately culture increasingly looks like the issue and takes time to change.

author: GavinSBaker
text:
I thought ChatGPT was their Pearl Harbor.

Nope.

Upon learning the Samsung search agreement was at risk employees “reacted with emojis and surprise. ‘Wow, OK, that’s wild,’ one responded.”

This is like being surprised at the Battle of Coral Sea five months after Pearl Harbor.

author: GavinSBaker
text:
3) From “they’ll be gone” to “lost in time, like tears in rain”

Eliminating the “little” helps build upon the similarities between Deckard and Roy at the end of the movie.

“As magnesium” isn’t a great simile.

I am no film critic but  Rutger Hauer’s rewrite was much better IMO.

author: GavinSBaker
text:
2) Original:

“I've seen things..seen things you little people wouldn't believe. Attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion bright as magnesium. I rode on the back decks of a blinker and watched C-beams glitter in the dark near the Tannhäuser Gate. All those moments..they'll

author: GavinSBaker
text:
1) Rutger Hauer rewrote/improvised this:

“I've seen things you people wouldn't believe..Attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion..I watched C-beams glitter in the dark near the Tannhäuser Gate. All those moments will be lost in time, like tears in rain..Time to die.”
